name: Velar
tagline: Memory and context substrate for production AI agents
investmentRationale: Organic developer adoption (612 stars, no press) before emergence from stealth is the rarest early-stage signal. Guy Podjarny LP network to the London AI research scene. Window: pre-announcement, ~4 weeks.

founders.name: Dr. Anya Sharma
founders.background: 4 years at Anthropic (Interpretability team), Imperial College PhD in ML, lead author on Anthropic's context-window extension research

founders.name: Tom Witzel
founders.background: ex-DeepMind Research Engineer, 3 published papers on memory-augmented transformers, contributor to EleutherAI

thesisNote: Enterprise agents fail in production because they have no persistent memory. Velar is building the primitive that makes them reliable. firstminute's own portfolio companies — Granola, n8n, Tessl — all face this exact problem today. A seed cheque here is positioning ahead of the infrastructure wave that follows the current agentic hype.
warmPath: Dr. Sharma spent 4 years at Anthropic Interpretability — Guy Podjarny (LP, Snyk co-founder) has direct relationships into the London AI research community and has already met Witzel at a London ML meetup. This is a one-email path.
action: Reach out via Guy Podjarny before this breaks out of stealth. A 20-minute call on the memory layer for agentic enterprise software. Window: 2-3 weeks before they raise.

signals.headline: Both co-founders updated LinkedIn to 'Co-founder at Stealth AI Infrastructure, London' simultaneously
signals.detail: Simultaneous stealth updates are the standard pre-emergence pattern. Both profiles removed company affiliations on the same evening (July 6, ~23:00 GMT). Tom Witzel also followed 3 firstminute portfolio founders the same week. Imminent public launch.

signals.headline: velar-core repository: 612 stars in 4 days, trending in AI-tools and DevOps
signals.detail: README describes 'a lightweight, embeddable memory substrate for LLM agents — no vector database required'. Clean Go architecture, 8 external contributors already, no press coverage yet. Organic developer adoption before any announcement is the strongest early signal available.

signals.headline: Dr. Anya Sharma's Anthropic profile went private; title changed to 'Founder'
signals.detail: Departure from Anthropic Interpretability is rare and always meaningful. Sharma was the lead author on Anthropic's context-window extension work. Engineers at that level leave only to build. GitHub commit history shows a new private org spun up on June 30.

signals.headline: Anonymous HN comment 'we are building something in this space, London-based' — 87 upvotes
signals.detail: User handle 'axshrmn' — consistent with Sharma's typical username pattern across GitHub and X. 87 upvotes and 14 replies asking for a waitlist. Pre-announcement community seeding.

name: Meridian
tagline: Autonomous compliance monitoring for the EU AI Act
investmentRationale: EU AI Act enforcement is a forced adoption event (Jan 2027 deadline). Regulatory insider founders are rare. No round open yet — use the next 3 months to build relationship ahead of the raise.

founders.name: Katrin Vogler
founders.background: 6 years at BaFin (German Federal Financial Supervisory Authority), AI policy lead. Left March 2026

founders.name: Priya Mehta
founders.background: ex-Palantir London (3 years, data infrastructure for financial regulators), ex-McKinsey FS practice

thesisNote: The EU AI Act enforcement timeline is real — large enterprises have 12 months to demonstrate compliance. No one has built a product-grade compliance layer yet. Meridian has a regulatory insider (BaFin) who knows what enforcement actually looks like from the inside. Europe-native, and the window to become the category default is 2026.
warmPath: Meridian is adjacent to Taktile (financial decision intelligence, firstminute portfolio). The Taktile founding team has been navigating the same EU regulatory landscape and can provide an intro. Larry Summers (Taktile investor, firstminute network) has spoken publicly about EU AI governance.
action: Intro via Taktile co-founders in Q3 2026. Frame as firstminute's portfolio already navigating EU AI Act compliance — natural fit for a Berlin-based regulatory AI company.

signals.headline: Katrin Vogler listed as speaker at EU AI Act implementation workshop, Berlin, July 2026
signals.detail: Conference speakers who just left regulators and are presenting at compliance workshops are almost always building. Talk title: 'What enterprises are actually getting wrong about the AI Act'. Classic pre-announcement positioning. Speaker bio lists 'independent advisor' — typical stealth cover.

signals.headline: Senior ML engineer (ex-AWS Berlin) connected to both founders within 48 hours of each other
signals.detail: Priya Mehta and Katrin Vogler both connected to Mehmet Yildiz (AWS ML Berlin, 5 years) within the same 48-hour window on July 1. Likely a first engineering hire in progress. Team formation accelerating.

signals.headline: meridian-ai.eu and meridian-compliance.com registered via Namecheap, privacy-protected
signals.detail: Two domains registered same day: .eu for the product, .com for the brand. Privacy protection enabled. This is Priya Mehta's standard pattern from Palantir — they always registered customer-facing domains first. Company formation likely imminent.

signals.headline: BaFin AI Policy Lead Katrin Vogler confirmed departure from supervisory authority
signals.detail: BaFin press release thanked Vogler for 'her contribution to the EU AI Act implementation framework'. Regulatory insiders who depart after landmark policy completions almost always commercialise their expertise. Six-year tenure means deep institutional relationships still in place.

name: Forge
tagline: AI-native code review that understands intent, not just diff
investmentRationale: Scott Chacon (LP, GitHub co-founder) knows Luca van den Berg personally from GitHub. Product Hunt #1 and HN frontpage validated PMF. Seed round not yet priced — get in before a tier-1 dev-tooling fund leads.

founders.name: Luca van den Berg
founders.background: ex-GitHub (5 years, engineering lead on Code Review product, built GitHub's first AI-assisted review suggestion feature)

founders.name: Sofia Rossi
founders.background: ex-Stripe (4 years, infra), built Stripe's internal code audit tooling used across 300+ engineers

thesisNote: Developer tooling with AI-native architecture is exactly where firstminute is positioned through Tessl and Codewords. Forge attacks the pull-request review problem — the daily friction point for every engineering team. Luca van den Berg built this from the inside at GitHub. Scott Chacon (LP) can validate the founding team's credibility directly.
thesisSource: firstminute portfolio thesis + Product Hunt data + HN
warmPath: Scott Chacon (LP, GitHub co-founder) knows Luca van den Berg from their overlapping time at GitHub. This is a direct, high-confidence introduction. The angel round already includes 2 GitHub alumni — natural co-investment with the LP community.
warmPathSource: firstminute LP database (Scott Chacon, GitHub co-founder)
action: Scott Chacon intro to Luca immediately. Seed round not yet priced. Position firstminute alongside the GitHub angel network before a tier-1 dev-tooling fund gets a look.

signals.headline: Forge launched on Product Hunt: #1 Product of the Day, 847 upvotes, 200+ comments
signals.detail: 247 comments, most from working engineers describing specific pain points Forge solves. 'This is the first code review tool that actually understands what I was trying to do, not just what I changed' — top comment with 94 upvotes. Product-market fit signal before a formal fundraise.

signals.headline: Show HN: Forge — we built what GitHub Copilot review should have been. 412 upvotes, front page 6 hours.
signals.detail: 412 upvotes on Show HN, 89 comments, front page for 6 hours. Top comment from a Stripe engineer: 'We've been using the beta for 3 weeks and our review cycle time dropped 40%.' Four engineering leads from Series B+ companies asking about enterprise pricing.

signals.headline: AngelList syndicate closed $800K from 14 GitHub and Stripe alumni angels
signals.detail: 14 individual angels, 9 with GitHub or Stripe backgrounds. Average cheque ~$57K. No lead investor yet — filled through Luca's personal network in 11 days. Seed pricing conversation expected August 2026.

name: Caelum
tagline: Real-time climate risk intelligence API from satellite and sensor data
investmentRationale: ESA BIC acceptance is strong institutional validation. CSRD enforcement (Jan 2027) is a forced-adoption catalyst. Too early for a firstminute cheque now — revisit Q4 2026 when they have first customer signal.

founders.name: Dr. Margot Lefebvre
founders.background: 5 years at ESA (European Space Agency), lead on Earth observation data pipeline for climate modelling, PhD in atmospheric science

founders.name: Ravi Krishnan
founders.background: ex-Palantir (4 years, climate data systems for European energy utilities), built carbon accounting pipelines for 3 national governments

thesisNote: Climate risk is moving from ESG reporting into operational risk management for financial institutions and infrastructure operators. Caelum is building the API layer that enables real-time, asset-level climate risk scoring. EU CSRD and TCFD mandates are creating forced adoption. Very early — no PitchBook entry, no press.
thesisSource: firstminute portfolio analysis + ESA BIC Paris announcement
warmPath: The ESA BIC Paris acceptance gives direct access to ESA's corporate partner network. Revisit Q4 2026 via ESA BIC intro. firstminute LP base includes several European operators with deep infrastructure exposure.
warmPathSource: ESA BIC Paris programme + firstminute LP network
action: Monitor until first customer signal (Q4 2026). Intro via ESA BIC Paris programme. Flag for the Founders Forum EU climate infrastructure session.

signals.headline: First public tweet from @caelum_ai: 'We're building the Bloomberg terminal for climate risk. API-first, real-time, asset-level.' — 1.2K likes
signals.detail: Account created July 4. First tweet went semi-viral in the climate-tech and ESG data communities. 34 quote tweets from climate data professionals, 4 from financial risk managers at tier-1 banks.

signals.headline: Caelum accepted into ESA BIC Paris incubator — 1 of 6 companies in the 2026 cohort
signals.detail: ESA BIC acceptance is selective (typically 6-8% acceptance rate) and includes access to ESA proprietary satellite datasets, €50K non-dilutive grant, and introductions to ESA's commercial partner network (Airbus, Thales, etc.).

signals.headline: Dr. Margot Lefebvre left ESA after 5 years; Ravi Krishnan left Palantir London same week
signals.detail: Synchronised departure from ESA and Palantir is a clear co-founding signal. Both updated profiles with 'building in stealth' on the same day.

signals.headline: 4 new LinkedIn connections in 2 weeks: atmospheric scientist, 2 backend engineers (ex-Datadog, ex-Snowflake), 1 enterprise sales
signals.detail: Classic pre-seed team assembly pattern. Enterprise sales hire with FSI background suggests early GTM focus on compliance buyers.

name: Delphi
tagline: Voice AI for EU healthcare intake — GDPR-native, multilingual
investmentRationale: Avoca (US analog) proves the vertical voice AI model. Delphi is the European version — GDPR-native, multilingual, 3 enterprise pilots confirmed. Seed likely Q3 2026 but not currently raising. Build relationship now via Munich LP network.

founders.name: Dr. Jonas Weber
founders.background: ex-Bupa (Head of Digital Health, 4 years), medical doctor turned product builder. Built Bupa's first automated intake triage system

founders.name: Emre Celik
founders.background: ex-Alan Health (engineering lead, 3 years), built Alan's voice symptom checker used by 120K patients monthly

thesisNote: Avoca (firstminute sourcing target) proved the vertical voice AI model for US services. Delphi is the European version of that thesis — GDPR-native, multilingual (German, French, English), specifically targeting healthcare intake where the pain is acute and the regulatory moat is defensible.
warmPath: Kleiner Perkins and General Catalyst backed Avoca in the US — the same LPs are now looking for a European equivalent. firstminute's existing relationship with these funds through Granola and Mistral creates a natural co-investment conversation.
warmPathSource: firstminute LP co-investor relationships (Kleiner, General Catalyst)
action: Direct approach via Munich LP network in Q3 2026. Reference Avoca as the US comp and position as the European entry.

signals.headline: Delphi cited in Handelsblatt deep-dive on AI in German healthcare: 'one of the most promising early-stage plays'
signals.detail: First press mention. Handelsblatt is Germany's leading business newspaper. Three other companies in the same article have since raised rounds.

signals.headline: 3 enterprise pilots confirmed: posts from 2 German GP clinics and 1 private hospital group
signals.detail: Unprompted customer posts from three separate clinical settings within the same week. One clinic noted 'intake time reduced from 18 minutes to 6 minutes'. Product-market fit evidence before a seed round.

signals.headline: €600K angel round from 8 Munich-based angels including 2 ex-Bupa board members and a health-tech founder
signals.detail: Quiet round, no press release. Sourced via German Startup Monitor database. Backer quality is high (2 ex-Bupa board members, 1 founder of iDoctus, acquired by Springer 2021). Seed round conversation likely Q3 2026.

name: Argus
tagline: Runtime security monitoring and policy enforcement for AI agent deployments
investmentRationale: Guy Podjarny co-founded Snyk and personally knows Ben Hartley (Snyk founding engineer #4). Round 60% committed, closes in 2-3 weeks. firstminute as strategic LP (Snyk connection) adds more value than any generic seed fund here.

founders.name: Ben Hartley
founders.background: Snyk founding engineer (#4 hire), built Snyk's dependency vulnerability scanner, left 2025 after Snyk IPO process. 3 years at Palo Alto Networks AI Security Research

founders.name: Yuki Tanaka
founders.background: ex-Cloudflare (security infrastructure, 4 years), built Cloudflare's bot detection ML system. Spoke at Black Hat 2025 on LLM prompt injection in production systems

thesisNote: Enterprise AI agent adoption is accelerating but security is an afterthought. Argus is building the runtime monitoring layer — what Snyk is to code, Argus is to AI agents. The founding team has done this before (Snyk #4 hire) and has the security community credibility to win developer trust. Guy Podjarny co-founded Snyk. He knows Ben Hartley personally.
thesisSource: firstminute portfolio thesis + BSides London demo + Argus GitHub
warmPath: Guy Podjarny co-founded Snyk and personally knows Ben Hartley (Snyk founding engineer #4). This is the warmest possible introduction — direct co-founder level trust. One email.
warmPathSource: firstminute LP database (Guy Podjarny, Snyk co-founder)
action: Guy Podjarny to Ben Hartley directly. Pre-seed round 60% committed — window to get in is 2-3 weeks. firstminute as a strategic LP (Snyk connection) adds more value than a generic seed fund here.

signals.headline: argus-runtime public repo: 340 stars, 12 external contributors, BSides London demo video linked
signals.detail: The repo went public July 6 alongside a BSides London demo. 340 stars in 48 hours, almost entirely from the security community. 12 external contributors already — a security product with this kind of community pull pre-launch is rare. Readme includes a 'we are raising' section.

signals.headline: Yuki Tanaka presented 'Argus: Runtime guardrails for LLM agents in production' — 400 attendees, 3 FTSE 100 CISOs in audience
signals.detail: BSides London is the practitioner security conference (not the PR circuit). A standing-room session with 400 attendees and a live demo of Argus catching a prompt injection attack in real time.

signals.headline: Ben Hartley left Palo Alto Networks AI Security Research; LinkedIn updated to 'Founder, building in London'
signals.detail: Hartley's departure from Palo Alto is significant — he spent 3 years post-Snyk building specific AI security research expertise before founding. LinkedIn post got 2,300 likes — the security community is watching.

signals.headline: Pre-seed round 60% committed: 3 Snyk alumni angels + 1 cybersecurity seed fund confirmed
signals.detail: Sourced via mutual connection in the London security community. $1.5M pre-seed, targeting close in July. Committed: 3 Snyk founding team angels (including one partner-level), 1 specialist cybersecurity seed fund. Gap to close: ~$600K. firstminute would be the lead.

name: Patchwork
tagline: Embedded finance infrastructure for European B2B SaaS
investmentRationale: Storyblok (firstminute portfolio, 13.32% stake) is a B2B SaaS company that could be a Patchwork customer — a warm commercial intro is available. Seed round in market now. Move in 3 weeks.

founders.name: Erik Lindqvist
founders.background: ex-Klarna (8 years, Head of B2B Payments Product), architected Klarna's B2B checkout product used by 40K merchants

founders.name: Amara Osei
founders.background: ex-Adyen (5 years, enterprise integrations), built Adyen's ISV/SaaS embedded payments toolkit

thesisNote: Every European B2B SaaS company eventually needs embedded payments, lending, and invoicing. Patchwork is building the Stripe for the European SaaS layer — with SEPA, SWIFT, and EU banking regulation built in from day one. The founding team has done this at Klarna and Adyen at scale. firstminute's Storyblok is an immediate reference customer.
warmPath: Storyblok (firstminute portfolio, 13.32% stake) is a B2B SaaS company that could be a Patchwork customer — a warm commercial intro is available. firstminute's Nordic LP network (Klarna alumni) adds direct social proof.
action: Storyblok intro as a commercial reference first, then a capital conversation. Validates both the product and firstminute's value-add beyond the cheque. Seed round in market now.

signals.headline: First enterprise customer announced: Stockholm-based HR SaaS processing €2M/mo through Patchwork
signals.detail: The customer (Teamtailor, Stockholm-based HR SaaS) posted on LinkedIn about Patchwork's embedded invoicing. €2M/month in payments processed in month 4 of the product. Real revenue signal before the seed round closes.

signals.headline: $1.2M angel round from 11 Klarna and Adyen alumni; Stripe Atlas entity registered
signals.detail: Affärsvärlden broke the angel round story. 11 angels from Klarna and Adyen — entirely operator-led, no institutional money yet. Stripe Atlas US entity registered alongside a Swedish AB — dual jurisdiction structure.

signals.headline: Patchwork hiring: Head of Partnerships (SaaS-focused) + Senior Backend Engineer (payments infra)
signals.detail: Head of Partnerships role specifically mentions 'building integrations with European SaaS platforms' — signals a go-to-market motion targeting the exact same companies firstminute backs.

name: Nimbus
investmentRationale: Isabel Mora is Tessian alumni — Tessian is the founding team behind Omnea (firstminute sourcing target). The Omnea team can make a direct warm introduction. Round currently raising at £800K, 312 waitlist signups in 48h.

founders.name: Isabel Mora
founders.background: ex-Tessian (product lead, 3 years, joined pre-Series A, stayed through Proofpoint acquisition 2023). Left 2025 to explore SMB finance pain points

founders.name: Kwame Asante
founders.background: ex-Revolut (4 years, Senior PM on Business Accounts product, owned the SMB invoicing feature used by 800K+ businesses)

thesisNote: SMB finance operations are still largely manual. Nimbus automates the AP/AR cycle end-to-end — invoice capture, matching, payment scheduling, reconciliation — using AI trained on European accounting formats and VAT structures. Adjacent to Omnea (procurement AI, firstminute sourcing target) and shares the compliance-AI thesis.
thesisSource: firstminute sourcing analysis + Companies House + LinkedIn
warmPath: Isabel Mora is a Tessian alumni — Tessian is the founding team behind Omnea (firstminute sourcing target). The Omnea team can make a direct introduction. This is a second-degree portfolio intro — high trust, zero friction.
action: Intro via Omnea founders to Isabel Mora. Pre-seed round in market at £800K. firstminute can anchor the round with LP network SMB operators as reference customers.

signals.headline: 'Nimbus is live — helping UK SMBs reclaim 6 hours a week on invoicing. Waitlist open.' — 1,800 likes, 312 signups in 48h
signals.detail: Isabel Mora's LinkedIn post announcing Nimbus got 1,800 likes — driven entirely by her network from Tessian, Proofpoint, and Revolut alumni. 312 waitlist signups within 48 hours. The Tessian founder community is actively cheering this on.

signals.headline: Kwame Asante left Revolut Business Accounts team after 4 years; updated to 'Co-founder at Nimbus'
signals.detail: Asante's post: 'After 4 years watching SMBs struggle with the same invoicing pain, I left to fix it.' 4,200 likes, 340 comments — highest-engagement departure post in the UK fintech community this month.

signals.headline: Nimbus Technologies Ltd registered at Companies House; nimbus.finance domain registered same day
signals.detail: Companies House registration and domain registered June 25. Two directors listed: Mora and Asante. £1 share capital (standard UK pre-seed formation). nimbus.finance — premium domain purchase signals confidence and long-term brand intent.

signals.headline: 18 SMB founders confirmed as early beta users in UK Fintech Founders Slack (2,400 members)
signals.detail: Isabel Mora posted in the UK Fintech Founders Slack asking for beta testers. 18 SMB founders signed up within the thread, with 4 posting positive feedback publicly. This community produces the best early B2B reference customers.
